_comment: Capability baseline for /capability-check (v14.6.0+). Snapshot of the Claude Code features and external deps this plugin currently USES or has deliberately considered. /capability-check diffs the LIVE Claude Code changelog/docs + dep versions against this file and reports candidate adoptions — it never self-applies. Maintainer updates this via `/capability-check --update-baseline` (a repo-root, committed action) after reviewing a report. The `product_directions` section is the home for /capability-check --strategy mode's scored, grounded, deduped product directions (each with status adopted | proposed | deferred); --strategy reads it to dedup (never re-proposing a recorded direction), and it is written only via `/capability-check --strategy --update-baseline`. NOTE: `baseline_date` + `plugin_version_at_last_scan` are the (date, plugin-version) pair of the last full platform/adoption scan — they record WHEN the `claude_code.*` lists were last refreshed, NOT the current plugin version. Both are bumped together only by a real `--update-baseline` adoption scan (Workflow A step 5). They are NOT bumped by `--strategy` direction edits: the `product_directions` section (introduced in v14.9.0) carries its own per-entry `date`s, so adding or restatusing a direction leaves the platform-scan pair untouched.

known_not_adopted.why: documented as the inbound reply leg for v14.1.0 notifications; not yet wired into any workflow
considered for state-flush before compaction; SessionStart resume covers the recovery path for now
documented as experimental/complementary; graduation criteria tracked in agent-teams skill, not met

sources_to_check.search_fallback: Claude Code changelog new features hooks tools

product_directions.title: System Twin
product_directions.rationale: Evolve from a stateless task→PR runner into a System Twin — accumulated project-specific state is the only durable moat ('rent a coder vs. grow an engineer') that a competitor can't copy and the platform won't build (the three integrated pillars are in pillars[]; see provenance for detail).
